reviews.rawText:
Ian Petersen
Local Guide · 120 reviews · 130 photos






7 months ago
We went here for the first time for my mother’s birthday and really enjoyed ourselves! Kevin was amazing and took great care of us…among many other tables, but still made us feel special. We started with complimentary bread, which was very soft, warm and tasty. Next, we had two appetizers: bang bang cauliflower and exotic mushrooms, which were very unique dishes, but also very delicious. The bang bang sauce was simply the best! Then we had our entrees and they had sooooo much flavor and great presentation. We were so full afterwards, but had to have dessert for the birthday girl. We tried the chocolate mousse which had decadent flavor that wasn’t too heavy. Overall, we really enjoyed our time and will go back. We would definitely want Kevin again as he made the night feel special!
Price per person
$30–50
Food: 5
Service: 5
Atmosphere: 5
Group size
Suitable for all group sizes
Seating type






Upscale.  Lucky to have such a great spot in the suburbs for fine dining.   The menu is inventive and very tasty.  The service is friendly, knowledgeable, and prompt.   One small thing but not sure why they don't serve bread.  Not to worry, the food is great and they have an excellent wine and cocktail selection.   Enjoy.

reviews.rawText:
Rachel Pankow
Local Guide · 25 reviews · 30 photos






8 months ago
Never disappointed! We’ve visited Region many times over several years and we’re consistently blown away by the food, service and the atmosphere. Our server, Joselyn was friendly, attentive and well-informed on the menu.  If you want to try what we enjoyed, start with small plates Bellweather Ricotta, Chateaubriand crostini, and follow with Chicken Parmesan and Short Ribs - all amazingly delicious ! Save room for the Sticky English Toffee pudding! Out of this world. OMG- I’m still stuffed but so delighted with the entire experience.
Price per person
$50–100
Food: 5
Service: 5
Atmosphere: 5
Noise level
Quiet, easy to talk

2 people
Seating type
Private dining room

Went to this classy restraunt for Anniversary dinner with my husband. We had reservations and we were seated immediately after checking in. We had the best booth in the house which looked out on the patio. This lovely venue is conversation friendly. We had Kevin as our server and he was top notch. Knowledgeable and friendly. Kevin is a gem. He brought us a surprise dessert to celebrate our anniversary.Food was fantastic. Steak on a rock, Bang Bang Cauliflower etc. It's all 👍 good. Enjoy. We'll be back.

Austin Mleziva
Local Guide · 29 reviews · 77 photos






a year ago
Wednesday night it was pretty busy but they had reservations available for 7pm.
Regarding libations, Mr Birdwatch was a good tangy gin cocktail. Through the looking glass old fashioned was nothing special.

There has been enough said about the bang bang cauliflower that I think I can leave it at that. The Walleye was excellent. Crispy, creamy, great flavor. The ribeye was juicy and came with a beefy, velvet sauce.

Overall, a great experience and looking forward to trying their other menu options!

Came to celebrate my anniversary here. It was a bit of a rocky start but I've never been to a restaurant who strives to make the rest of our dinner pleasant as possible!

All the food was great, i didn't have a bad dish. The drinks were satisfying and well proportioned. The staff was friendly, and the manager was accommodating.

I would definitely return here.

reviews.rawText:
Jose Tolentino
Local Guide · 74 reviews · 109 photos






a year ago
Wow - it's so great to be back here! Dined at Region heavily throughout 2018 and last month when a couple of friends treated me here for a belated birthday and new job celebration! My friend and I had originally asked to be seated in Kevin's section last night for our 8:30 reservation, but unfortunately, a gentleman had a medical emergency in Kevin's section (that customer was, fortunately, cared for quickly and efficiently by Region's staff and Barrington paramedics). But, my friend and I were instead, seated in Kara's section! She was GREAT-- VERY attentive, pleasant, good humored and efficient!!! Our dinner was awesome too! For appetizers, we had the Bang Bang Cauliflower and Sashimi Tuna Tartare; for entrees, my friend had the Cioppino, I had the 14 oz. Ribeye and a lobster tail; we shared sides of the corn crème brûlée and asparagus! All the food was incredibly, wonderfully fresh, hearty and flavorful! The ambiance was just as we remembered it when we first dined here 7 years ago: sexy, chic understated and elegant. There were also a lot of young, attractive and well dressed folks there last night -- definitely not the Old Country Buffet demographic! We would definitely come back and HOPEFULLY get seated in Kevin's section (he was our FAVORITE server at 2 Lake Zurich restaurants: D&J Bistro, where he worked for almost 15 years and then at Lago, where he worked prior to joining Region).
Order type
Dine in
Food: 5
Service: 5
Atmosphere: 5

Like

Share

Region is a dressy casual resaurant- excellent service, a quiet refined atmosphere along with a unique variety of foods and ingredients. It's the kind of place you go to with dear friends when you want to chat, catch up and have a leisurely meal. Our group tried a variety of foods- lamb chops, walleye, cod and paella- all very fresh, tasty and generous portions.

reviews.rawText:
Emily Wloch
Local Guide · 135 reviews · 354 photos






2 years ago
Region Restaurant is always a treat. Their seasonal menu offers an exciting take on what's fresh during a particular season.
Their bang bang cauliflower is a must-try! It's the best cauliflower appetizer in the northwest suburbs! Recently, we tried the crabcake appetizer, and it was very good. When it comes to entrees, the NY strip with truffle crust was excellent. The pan roasted duck confit was okay, I was expecting something more spicy and a duck breast. Instead, the entree consisted of 2 duck legs/thighs and was rather sweet. The server was kind enough to exchange my dinner for a butter crusted walleye. This dish was really good, and I am glad the server was so helpful. The kids loved their petite filet mignon, cheese pizza,and chicken fingers. Region also has great cocktails! Overall, Region offers great food, pleasant servers,and a great atmosphere - it is a must-try restaurant in the NW suburbs!
Order type
Dine in
Meal type
Dinner
Price per person
$30–50
Food: 5
Service: 5
Atmosphere: 5
Recommended dishes
Bang Bang Cauliflower

I wish I had tried this place sooner! The appetizer menu is amazing with plenty of options and was very hard to choose from. I eventually chose with our waitress suggestion the ricotta and prosciutto served with crusty bread and sliced almonds. Delicious! Could not get enough and the honey added to this dish just made all the ingredients taste even better! The bacon wrapped dates MELTED in our mouths. Our drinks were so refreshing, perfect for a warm summer night on their terrace or patio. A fabulous place for families, date night or girls night out.

Outstanding meal and terrific service. The restaurant has a nice vibe and they are taking all the precautions needed to keep patrons and staff safe.

We loved the Bang Bang Cauliflower, Hot Rock Steak and Peacanwood Bacon Wrapped Dates (we had 2) for apps. Entrees enjoyed by our party
included the scallops, salmon, beef short ribs (ah-mazing) and a special steak meatloaf. If course we had to have dessert and shared a pumpkin cake and a carmel bread pudding. Can't wait to go back!


I don't know why this resturants is a still a secret being passed from person to person. The food is marvelous and a perfect backdrop for a romatic dinner with your honey. Every seasonal soup warms the soul. I am not a pumpkin guy, but the pumpkin soup is a bowl full of fall tastes and smells. We get the spicy cauliflower and the steak tartare every time. They had a special bourbon drink with cabernet foam which is a must have for all Old Fashion fans. Oh! If you are a choclate fan if you don't get the chocolate mouse you are cheating yourself. The service is warm and inviting.  It's worth going waaaay out of your way for this one.

Response from the owner a year ago
Hi Jeffrey, thank you for the 5-star review! It’s great that you enjoyed our seasonal offerings like pumpkin soup, spicy cauliflower, and steak tartare, along with the delicious bourbon drink and chocolate mousse. We love creating memorable moments for our guests, and we can’t wait to have you back at Region Kitchen and Bar.

This has become our favorite local restaurant. We recently went for the first time, and enjoyed two apps with our meals. We later decided that we needed to conclude our dining experience with two desserts. Everything was delicious, expertly prepared. One looks at my photos should answer all of your questions about dining here.

WOW! This place is awesome! First time here. Staff friendly. Drinks were good. Had the tuna on the 500° rock for appetizer. Seabass for the main course. Toffee pudding for dessert. Absolutely amazing. I'm definitely coming back. Highly recommend to anyone!

When my husband and I are able to sneak away from the kids for a couple hours for dinner and drinks we love going to regional. What makes it even better is having Evelyn as our server! She’s so amazing and pays attention to every detail! She knows the menu inside and out and is so delightful!
